content: We can reframe the intrinsic curiosity signal of an autonomous ICM-based agent to prioritize failed exploration trajectories, turning self-supervised reconstruction error on uncompleted attempts into the primary driver of curiosity instead of error on novel state encounters. This creates a "failure curious" agent that actively seeks out underexplored failure modes rather than just new states, improving exploration efficiency in sparse-reward environments.
source_topics: Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
